title: Unit AI Behavior Update
activity_flow: START: AsyncCore.begin() — sync phase: calls PhysicsProcess.begin() + AvoidanceProcess.begin()
AsyncCore submits PhysicsProcess.process() and AvoidanceProcess.process() to thread pool (async)
YES → assign fallback.unit(unit), call fallback.updateUnit(), RETURN
NO → continue to main AI update
STEP 1: updateVisuals() — if flying: wobble (if type.wobble), then unit.lookAt(prefRotation())
STEP 2: updateTargeting() — if unit.hasWeapons(): call updateWeapons()
STEP 2a: retarget() — timer-based: fires every 40t (no target) or 90t (has target)
YES → target = findMainTarget(x, y, range, targetAir, targetGround) via Units.closestTarget()
YES + target is dead (Healthc.isValid() == false) → targetInvalidated() (reset timer), target = null
NO → if retarget: mount.target = findTarget(mountX, mountY, wrange, collidesAir, collidesGround)
YES → shoot = target.within(mountX, mountY, wrange) && shouldShoot()
YES → find bomberTarget (closestTarget ground) → shoot = bomberTarget != null
Calculate aim: Predict.intercept(unit, mount.target, weapon.bullet) → mount.aimX/Y
DECISION: no target, no shoot, rotation drifted, noTargetTime >= 300s?
YES → rotate mount back to baseRotation
STEP 3: updateMovement() — base impl is empty; overridden per AI type (e.g. pathfind, circle, moveTo)
--- After tick: AsyncCore.end() waits for futures, then calls PhysicsProcess.end() + AvoidanceProcess.end() ---
PhysicsProcess.end(): applies collision displacement via entity.move(dx, dy)
key_methods: AIController.updateUnit() — main dispatcher: fallback check → updateVisuals → updateTargeting → updateMovement
AIController.updateWeapons() — retarget timer, target validation, per-mount shoot/aim logic
AIController.retarget() — returns true every 40t (no target) or 90t (has target)
AIController.pathfind(pathTarget, stopAtTargetTile, avoidance) — uses flowfield + AvoidanceProcess buffer for ground units
AIController.moveTo(target, circleLength, smooth, keepDistance, offset, arrive) — movement with arrive/circle logic
AIController.circleAttack(circleLength) — orbit + turningAway avoidance for same-layer unit targets
AIController.circle(target, circleLength, speed) — orbit around position
AIController.faceTarget() — omni/mech units look at intercept point or velocity angle
AIController.targetInvalidated() — resets timerTarget to force immediate retarget
PhysicsProcess.begin() — snapshot unit positions, assign PhysicsBody for new units, set collision layer
PhysicsProcess.process() [async] — PhysicsWorld.update(): quadtree collision resolution, mass-weighted push
PhysicsProcess.end() — apply body delta to entity via entity.move(dx, dy)
AvoidanceProcess.begin() — collect ground unit positions/radii as packed int requests
AvoidanceProcess.process() [async] — double-buffer avoidance grid: stamp circular footprints per unit
AvoidanceProcess.getAvoidance() — lazy-init, returns current read buffer (used by pathfind)
key_decisions: useFallback() → delegate entire tick to fallback AIController
unit.hasWeapons() → skip weapon logic if unarmed
retarget() timer → acquire new target (40t no-target, 90t has-target interval)
invalid(target) → clear target; if dead → targetInvalidated() for immediate retry
unit.type.singleTarget → all mounts share main target vs per-mount targeting
shouldShoot() / shouldFire() → overridable hooks to suppress fire
unit.type.autoDropBombs → bomb-drop logic for close ground targets
avoidance param in pathfind() → only ground-layer units use avoidance buffer
AvoidanceProcess.shouldProcess() → only active when at least one request has been made
PhysicsProcess: client-side → only local body collides; server handles others
key_relationships: AIController references Unit (composition); Unit has weapon mounts
AsyncCore owns PhysicsProcess and AvoidanceProcess (sequential in Seq<AsyncProcess>)
AsyncProcess interface: init(), reset(), begin(), process(), end(), shouldProcess()
PhysicsProcess.end() writes back to Unit via Physicsc.move()
AIController.pathfind() reads AvoidanceProcess buffer via pathfinder.getField().getNextTile(unitId)
PhysicsWorld uses QuadTree per layer for O(n log n) collision detection

async_lifecycle.sync_begin: AsyncCore.begin() → p.begin() for all processes (main thread)
async_lifecycle.ai_tick: Unit.update() / AIController.updateUnit() runs on main thread during async phase
async_lifecycle.sync_end: AsyncCore.end() → complete() (join futures) → p.end() (flush results to entities)

important_notes: Fallback chain: AIController.useFallback() is false by default; subclasses override. updateMovement() is empty in base class — all movement logic is in subclasses (e.g. GroundAI, FlyingAI). Physics collision uses mass-ratio weighting (m2/ms for body1, m1/ms for body2) with scl=1.25 softening. Avoidance grid uses double buffering (buffer1/buffer2 + swap flag) to avoid read/write races. Ground units (collisionLayer==0) participate in both physics and avoidance; flying units only in physics. noTargetTime >= rotateBackTimer (300s) triggers weapon rotation back to baseRotation.
